codeReview.summary: The commit successfully reconciles legacy docs to the new mainline canonical frontend structure. Five files changed: (1) docs/INDEX.md updated with a new 'Canonical Frontend Documentation' section pointing to docs/frontend-portals/ and docs/frontend-api/ as authoritative, and a 'Legacy Frontend Handoff (Reference Only)' section marking old handoff files as superseded; (2) docs/adr/ADR-001-allowlist-cross-module-imports-baseline.md deleted (duplicate of docs/adrs/); (3-5) NON-CANONICAL banners added to frontend-handoff-platform.md, frontend-handoff-operations.md, and frontend-handoff-commercial.md. All cross-links resolve to existing files. The lint-knowledgebase and enterprise-policy checks both passed. One non-blocking observation: docs/frontend-handoff-finance.md and docs/accounting-portal-frontend-engineer-handoff.md are listed in the 'Legacy Frontend Handoff (Reference Only)' section of INDEX.md but do not carry inline NON-CANONICAL banners in their file bodies. INDEX.md clearly marks them as reference-only, which is sufficient for the stated feature scope, but adding inline banners to these two files would improve consistency.

issues.description: Listed in INDEX.md 'Legacy Frontend Handoff (Reference Only)' section but lacks an inline NON-CANONICAL banner at the top of the file body. The three other frontend-handoff files (platform, operations, commercial) all received inline banners. Adding one here would be consistent, though INDEX.md's explicit 'Reference Only' labeling covers the requirement.

Listed in INDEX.md 'Legacy Frontend Handoff (Reference Only)' section but lacks an inline NON-CANONICAL banner. The file still reads as an active handoff document without any deprecation marker in its body.

sharedStateObservations.observation: docs/workflows/ and docs/code-review/ directories contain documents with NON-CANONICAL banners from prior reconciliation work, but are not listed in docs/INDEX.md at all. These are orphaned doc trees that are not navigable from the canonical index. The feature description says 'add useful orphaned docs/directories to index/navigation by default' but these were not addressed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: docs/workflows/ has 8 files with NON-CANONICAL banners; docs/code-review/ has 11 files including README.md. Neither directory appears in docs/INDEX.md. grep -c 'NON-CANONICAL' on these directories confirms banners exist.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The NON-CANONICAL banner pattern is well-established in the codebase (docs/CONVENTIONS.md documents it at line 137-149), but there is no automated check that legacy docs listed as reference-only in INDEX.md actually carry the inline banner. This led to the inconsistency where finance and accounting-portal handoff files are marked reference-only in INDEX.md but lack inline banners.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: docs/CONVENTIONS.md lines 137-149 define the NON-CANONICAL banner template. Three of five legacy frontend-handoff files have inline banners; two do not.

summary: PASS. The feature successfully reconciles legacy frontend docs to the mainline canonical structure. INDEX.md now clearly distinguishes canonical (docs/frontend-portals/, docs/frontend-api/) from legacy (frontend-handoff-*.md) frontend documentation, the duplicate docs/adr/ tree is removed in favor of docs/adrs/, and three of the five legacy handoff files carry inline NON-CANONICAL banners. Two non-blocking issues noted: (1) docs/frontend-handoff-finance.md and docs/accounting-portal-frontend-engineer-handoff.md lack inline NON-CANONICAL banners despite being listed as reference-only in INDEX.md, and (2) docs/workflows/ and docs/code-review/ are orphaned doc trees not reachable from INDEX.md.
